title: cleome quinquenervia var. noeana
intro: Cleome quinquenervia var. noeana, also known as the Noe's beeplant or bee plant, is a plant species within the Cleome genus and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, and has been utilized to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits associated with Cleome quinquenervia var. noeana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, have been traditionally used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
